headline: Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)
description: OOP organizes code around objects (data + behavior) rather than functions. Four pillars: Encapsulation (hide internals), Inheritance (reuse through hierarchy), Polymorphism (same interface, different behavior), Abstraction (simplify complex reality). Languages: Java, C++, Python, C#, Ruby.

citation.name: Object-Oriented Analysis and Design with Applications (4th Edition, 2025)
citation.author: Booch G., Maksimchuk R., Engle M., Young B., Conallen J., Houston K.

citation.name: OOP in the Age of AI: Evolution or Extinction? (2025 Survey)
citation.author: multiple
